questions.question: What three gaps did the original Qwen-VL target versus LLaVA-1.5 and BLIP-2?
options: Price, latency, license
Resolution (336px is useless for invoices and dense screenshots), video (per-frame stacking fails on multi-minute footage), and structured output (agents need JSON, not free-form text)
Bigger context, more languages, fewer parameters
Speech, music, and 3D
questions.explanation: Every Qwen-VL generation extends one of these axes. Gen 1 (Aug 2023) shipped 448px, coordinate-token grounding ("<box>(112,204),(280,344)</box>"), and Chinese+English training — the grounding supervision was the headline.

questions.question: What architectural changes made Qwen2-VL the decisive generation?
options: A 10x bigger language model only
Native dynamic resolution (any HxW divisible by 28, no resize/tiling), M-RoPE 3D positions replacing position tables, an MLP projector replacing the Q-Former, and dynamic-FPS video
Switching from Python to Rust
Quantization to 4-bit
questions.explanation: Result: Qwen2-VL-7B matched GPT-4o on several benchmarks and beat it on DocVQA (94.5 vs 88.4). The rest of the open ecosystem copied each bet within twelve months.

questions.question: Why does M-RoPE need all three axes (temporal, height, width)?
options: For RGB channels
Images need 2D spatial position (t=0); video frames additionally need time — rotating Q/K by per-axis frequencies handles any resolution AND any frame count with no embedding table
One axis per attention head type
The third axis is for batch index
questions.explanation: 3D (t, h, w) positions unify still images and video under one scheme. MRoPE-v2 later scaled positions with max input size so 10-minute videos don't exhaust the frequency range.

questions.question: What did Qwen2.5-VL's absolute time tokens change about video understanding?
options: Videos play faster during training
Frames carry actual timestamps (<time>0.04</time>) instead of indices, so the model can say "at 0:04 the cat jumps" and dynamic FPS (1 for slow footage, 4+ for action) stays temporally grounded
Time zones are normalized to UTC
Frame order becomes irrelevant
questions.explanation: Positional indices break when FPS varies — frame 12 means nothing if sampling density changes. Absolute time tokens decouple event localization from the sampling rate.

questions.question: How did Qwen-VL make models "agent-ready out of the box"?
options: By shipping a separate agent SDK
Training on explicit JSON output formats including tool calls like {"tool": "click", "coords": [380, 220]} — structured output as a first-class training target alongside OCR and grounding
By removing all safety filters
Through a special agent license
questions.explanation: Computer-use agents need parseable coordinates and tool calls, not prose. Making structured output a training objective (not a prompting trick) is part of why the family dominates screen-agent benchmarks.
